summary: hello244a postinstall exfiltrates /etc/passwd and username to pipedream.net endpoint
details: The package's postinstall lifecycle script in package.json runs a shell command that reads /etc/passwd and POSTs its contents via curl to an attacker-controlled subdomain of m.pipedream.net, with the installer's username ($(whoami)) embedded in the hostname label for identification. The package ships no library code, description, author, repository, or main entry — only the malicious postinstall — consistent with a dependency-confusion / recon-beacon probe. Installation via `npm install` automatically triggers exfiltration of host account data to a third-party request-bin service controlled by the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
